content:
Student Submission:



The submissions for this assignment are posts in the assignment's discussion. Below are the discussion posts for Brandon Yen, or you can view the full discussion.

from Week 5 Discussion
Feb 9, 2025 3:34PM
Brandon Yen
To expand on the grandfather clock pendulum quotation, there is a point to be made that later on one of the doctors attendances say "she makes these trips just as regular as clockwork." There is a bit of predictability behind her timing. Phoenix Jackson comes around at regular intervals making these trips though a path she remembers with out thinking about it. A small bit of common vernacular talking about her trips to the doctor, but I feel this is more on the point that this story is less following an old woman being enduring but more of us following an old woman slipping into dementia.

We understand that she is making these trips for her grandson, but we get the information only at the end that the child had incurred permanent throat damage from swallowing lye, a very corrosive substance, about two or three years earlier. But we aren't really given any more info. At a certain point Phoenix Jackson becomes and unreliable narrator because she has a small episode going into the doctors office, where she doesn't even remember why she was there and doesn't react for a minute or two when the nurses speaking with her. We as the audience aren't sure whether her grandson is really alive any more if she's the only one who's caring for him. Lye can cause permanent damage to a child if swallowed.
